title: Hanuman Chalisa
description: The Hanuman Chalisa is a devotional hymn of 40 verses in praise of Hanuman, the divine monkey deity and devotee of Rama. Composed by Tulsidas in the 16th century, it is one of the most popular Hindu devotional texts.
bandcampFallbackLabel: Hanuman Chalisa - हनुमान चालीसा by Víctor Sakshin

translations.pt: Com a poeira dos pés de lótus do venerável Guru
translations.en: With the dust of the lotus feet of the revered Guru

translations.pt: Purifico o espelho do meu coração
translations.en: I purify the mirror of my heart

verses.original: बरनउँ रघुबर बिमल जसु जो दायकु फल चारि
verses.transliteration: baraṇa'u̐ raghubara bimala jasu jō dāyaku phala chāri

translations.pt: E narro o puro louvor de Raghubar (Rama), que concede os quatro frutos (dharma, artha, kama, moksha)
translations.en: And narrate the pure glory of Raghubar (Rama), who bestows the four fruits (dharma, artha, kama, moksha)
